# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent postgres weak auth.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf - Production Configuration
# =============================================================
# IMPORTANT: Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom, first match wins.
# All connections require strong authentication (scram-sha-256).
# The "trust" and "password" (plaintext) methods are NEVER used.
# =============================================================

# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# LOCAL CONNECTIONS (Unix domain socket)
# -------------------------------------------------------------

# Deny all local trust/peer connections by default.
# Only the postgres superuser can connect locally via peer (OS user must match).
local   all             postgres                                peer

# Application user can connect locally with scram-sha-256
local   app_db          app_user                                scram-sha-256

# Admin user can connect locally with scram-sha-256
local   all             admin_user                              scram-sha-256

# Readonly user can connect locally with scram-sha-256
local   app_db          readonly_user                           scram-sha-256

# Reject all other local connections explicitly
local   all             all                                     reject

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# IPv4 HOST-BASED CONNECTIONS (TCP/IP)
# -------------------------------------------------------------

# Loopback - only admin and app users, always encrypted auth
host    all             admin_user      127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    app_db          app_user        127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    app_db          readonly_user   127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256

# Application servers subnet (adjust to your actual app server CIDR)
# Only app_user can connect from application tier
hostssl app_db          app_user        10.10.1.0/24            scram-sha-256  clientcert=verify-ca

# Admin connections restricted to management/bastion subnet
# Require both SSL client certificate AND scram-sha-256 password
hostssl all             admin_user      10.10.0.0/28            scram-sha-256  clientcert=verify-full

# Readonly/reporting connections from analytics subnet
# Restricted to app_db only, require SSL + scram-sha-256
hostssl app_db          readonly_user   10.10.2.0/24            scram-sha-256  clientcert=verify-ca

# Monitoring system (e.g., pgbouncer health checks from known host)
hostssl postgres        monitoring_user 10.10.0.50/32           scram-sha-256  clientcert=verify-ca

# Replication connections from known replica servers only
# Each replica is explicitly listed by IP (no broad CIDR)
hostssl replication     repl_user       10.10.3.10/32           scram-sha-256  clientcert=verify-full
hostssl replication     repl_user       10.10.3.11/32           scram-sha-256  clientcert=verify-full

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# IPv6 HOST-BASED CONNECTIONS (TCP/IP)
# -------------------------------------------------------------

# Loopback IPv6
host    all             admin_user      ::1/128                 scram-sha-256
host    app_db          app_user        ::1/128                 scram-sha-256
host    app_db          readonly_user   ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

# -------------------------------------------------------------
# DENY-ALL CATCH-ALL RULES (must be last)
# -------------------------------------------------------------

# Reject everything else on IPv4
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject

# Reject everything else on IPv6
host    all             all             ::/0                    reject

# =============================================================
# CONFIGURATION NOTES (for operations team):
# =============================================================
#
# 1. AUTHENTICATION METHOD:
#    - scram-sha-256 is used exclusively (never md5, password, or trust)
#    - Ensure postgresql.conf has: password_encryption = 'scram-sha-256'
#
# 2. SSL/TLS REQUIREMENTS:
#    - All remote connections use "hostssl" (plaintext TCP rejected)
#    - Ensure postgresql.conf has:
#        ssl = on
#        ssl_cert_file = '/etc/postgresql/ssl/server.crt'
#        ssl_key_file = '/etc/postgresql/ssl/server.key'
#        ssl_ca_file = '/etc/postgresql/ssl/ca.crt'
#        ssl_min_protocol_version = 'TLSv1.3'
#
# 3. CLIENT CERTIFICATES:
#    - clientcert=verify-full: validates cert AND checks CN matches username
#    - clientcert=verify-ca: validates cert is signed by trusted CA
#    - Admin and replication require verify-full (strongest)
#
# 4. NETWORK SEGMENTATION:
#    - 10.10.0.0/28  = Management/bastion hosts (admin only)
#    - 10.10.1.0/24  = Application server tier
#    - 10.10.2.0/24  = Analytics/reporting tier
#    - 10.10.3.0/24  = Replication tier (individual IPs pinned)
#    - Adjust CIDRs to match your actual network topology
#
# 5. USER ROLES (create in PostgreSQL):
#    - admin_user:      Full privileges, MFA recommended at app layer
#    - app_user:        CONNECT on app_db, minimal DML privileges
#    - readonly_user:   SELECT-only on specific schemas/tables
#    - monitoring_user: pg_monitor role, SELECT on pg_stat views
#    - repl_user:       REPLICATION privilege only
#    - postgres:        OS-level peer auth only, never remote
#
# 6. AFTER CHANGES:
#    - Reload config: SELECT pg_reload_conf(); or pg_ctl reload
#    - Verify: SELECT * FROM pg_hba_file_rules;
#    - Test each role's connectivity before deploying
#
# =============================================================
